channel.title: Devblogs.microsoft.com on .NET Ramblings
channel.description: Recent content in Devblogs.microsoft.com on .NET Ramblings

item.title: Governing MCP tool calls in .NET with the Agent Governance Toolkit
item.description: AI agents are connecting to real tools — reading files, calling APIs, querying databases — through the Model Context Protocol (MCP). The Agent Governance Toolkit (AGT) provides a governance layer for these agent systems, en Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/dotnet/governing-mcp-tool-calls-in-dotnet-with-the-agent-governance-toolkit

item.title: VSTest is Removing its Newtonsoft.Json Dependency
item.description: Starting in .NET 11 Preview 4 and Visual Studio 18.8, VSTest, the platform that powers dotnet test and Test Explorer, will no longer depend on Newtonsoft.Json. The platform will now use System.Text.Json on .NET and JSONite on .NET Framework. This is a servicing and security change. Most projects require no action. A small number of Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/dotnet/vs-test-is-removing-its-newtonsoft-json-dependency

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.118
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.118 Read the full article Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/vscode-blog/visual-studio-code-1.118

item.title: General Availability: Refresh Token (RT) Transfer to Apple Watch in Microsoft Entra External ID Native Authentication
item.description: We’re excited to announce the General Availability (GA) of Single Sign-On (SSO) from Native Apps to Embedded Web Views for Microsoft Entra External ID (EEID) Native Authentication. This release marks a major milestone in delivering end-to-end seamless authentication experiences for modern CIAM applications bridging the gap between Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/identity/native-auth-refresh-token-transfer-ga

item.title: SDK-Style Support for Extension Projects
item.description: Starting in Visual Studio 18.5, you can create and build Visual Studio extensions (VSIX) using an officially supported SDK-style project. This brings VSIX projects into the modern build and deployment pipeline, improving incremental build performance and making the build → deploy → debug workflow more reliable. Install the Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/visualstudio/sdk-style-support-for-extension-projects

item.title: The New Loop: How Power Pages Builds Software with AI
item.description: Introduction When I started writing software professionally in 2015, the tech landscape looked completely different. Tools and frameworks kept evolving since then, but the process of building software barely moved for a decade. That changed in the last couple of months. Here's what has changed in how the Power Pages team builds an Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/powerplatform/the-new-loop-how-power-pages-builds-software-with-ai

item.title: A2A v1 Is Here: Cross-Platform Agent Communication in Microsoft Agent Framework for .NET
item.description: As organizations move from single-agent prototypes to multi-agent production systems, the ability for agents to communicate reliably across platforms and organizational boundaries becomes essential. With the release of A2A Protocol v1.0 and updated support in the Microsoft Agent Framework, you can now connect and expose your AI agents using a Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/agent-framework/a2a-v1-is-here-cross-platform-agent-communication-in-microsoft-agent-framework-for-net

item.title: Visual Studio April Update – Cloud Agent Integration
item.description: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio is becoming more agentic with every release. This update brings cloud agent integration front and center, letting you start remote coding sessions without leaving the IDE. Custom agents now support user-level definitions that travel with you across projects, C++ code editing tools for agent mode are generally av Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/visualstudio/visual-studio-april-update-cloud-agent-integration

item.title: The problem: All-or-nothing batch processing in Azure Service Bus
item.description: Azure Service Bus is one of the most widely used messaging services for building event-driven applications on Azure. When you use Azure Functions with a Service Bus trigger in batch mode, your function receives multiple messages at once for efficient, high-throughput processing. But what happens when one message in the batch fails? Assume y Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/azure-sdk/per-message-settlement-azure-service-bus

item.title: SharePoint Framework (SPFx) roadmap update – April 2026
item.description: April continues the strong momentum for the SharePoint Framework and the broader Microsoft 365 extensibility ecosystem. Over the past month, we have been making steady progress across the SPFx roadmap, refining delivery plans, connecting with the community in person, and preparing the next wav Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/microsoft365dev/sharepoint-framework-spfx-roadmap-update-april-2026

item.title: Welcome to SkiaSharp 4.0 Preview 1
item.description: Created 10 years ago, SkiaSharp has long been the backbone of cross-platform 2D graphics in .NET. It powers graphics rendering across mobile, desktop, web, and server targets with consistent, high-quality output using the open-source Skia engine. SkiaS Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/dotnet/welcome-to-skia-sharp-40-preview1

item.title: High-Performance Distributed Caching with .NET and Postgres on Azure
item.description: In the world of modern .NET development, applications need to maintain high throughput, scale rapidly, and most importantly, deliver performance and responsiveness in all circumstances. This becomes increasingly difficult as functional complexity increases, data sources expand, and we contend with ever-evolving endpoints and microservices. Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/dotnet/high-performance-distributed-caching-dotnet-postgres-azure

item.title: Developing a cross-process reader/writer lock with limited readers, part 1: A semaphore
item.description: Say you want to have the functionality of a reader/writer lock, but have it work cross-process. The built-in SRWLOCK works only within a single process. Can we build a reader/writer lock that works across processes? For convenience, let's say that you want to support a maximum of N simultaneous readers, for s Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/oldnewthing/20260428-00/?p=112278

item.title: Welcome to Azure Cosmos DB Conf 2026
item.description: Today is the day. Azure Cosmos DB Conf 2026, in partnership with AMD, is a free virtual developer event focused on building modern, scalable applications with Azure Cosmos DB. The Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/cosmosdb/welcome-to-azure-cosmos-db-conf-2026

item.title: PowerToys 0.99 is here: new monitor controls, easier window management, and Dock upgrades
item.description: Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/commandline/powertoys-0-99-is-here-new-monitor-controls-easier-window-management-and-dock-upgrades

item.title: Python Environments Extension for VS Code- April Update
item.description: Python Environments — April 2026 Release We're excited to announce the latest update to the Python Environments extension for Visual Studio Code. This release focuses on startup performance, reliability, Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/python/python-in-visual-studio-code-april-2026-release

item.title: Azure Data Studio is retired: Move your Azure SQL workflow to VS Code in 10 minutes
item.description: Azure Data Studio (ADS) retired on February 6, 2025, and support ended on February 28, 2026. The recommended path forward is Visual Studio Code with the MSSQL extension. If you used ADS daily, this guide gets you productive quickly in VS Code. The focus is on importing your existing setup, restoring familiar shortcuts like F5, and getting SQL Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/azure-sql/azure-data-studio-is-retired-move-your-azure-sql-workflow-to-vs-code-in-10-minutes

item.title: Announcing Shader Model 6.10 Preview and AgilitySDK 720 Preview
item.description: Overview Today, we are pleased to announce that Shader Model 6.10 and other features have been officially released with Agility SDK 1.720-preview and complementary DXC 1.10.2605.2. AgilitySDK 1.720-preview exposes the following features. There's more de Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/directx/shader-model-6-10-agilitysdk-720-preview

item.title: D3D12 LinAlg Matrix Preview
item.description: Welcome to the D3D12 LinAlg Matrix Preview release! Today, we are excited to announce the preview release for the D3D12 Linear Algebra APIs! This feature set unlocks comprehensive hardware acceleration for Matrix-oriented operations across various use cases. Previously, we announced the Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/directx/d3d12-linalg-preview

item.title: Looking at consequences of passing too few register parameters to a C function on various architectures
item.description: In our exploration of calling conventions for various processors on Windows, we learned that in many cases, some of the parameters are passed in registers. Suppose that there is a function that takes two parameters, but you know that the function ignores the second parameter if the first parameter is positive. What happens if you ca Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/oldnewthing/20260427-00/?p=112271

item.title: Azure MCP Server now available as an MCP Bundle (.mcpb)
item.description: We're excited to announce that the Azure MCP Server is now available as an MCP Bundle (.mcpb). This means you can install the Azure MCP Server into Claude Desktop and other MCP-compatible clients Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/azure-sdk/azure-mcp-server-mcpb-support

item.title: Chat History Storage Patterns in Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description: When people talk about building AI agents, they usually focus on models, tools, and prompts. In practice, one of the most important architectural decisions is much simpler: where does the conversation history live? Imagine a user asks your agent a complex question, clicks “try again,” explores tw Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/agent-framework/chat-history-storage-patterns-in-microsoft-agent-framework

item.title: Defending against exceptions in a scope_exit RAII type
item.description: One of the handy helpers in the Windows Implementation Library (WIL) is wil::scope_exit. We've used it to Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/oldnewthing/20260424-00/?p=112266

item.title: Axios npm Supply Chain Compromise – Guidance for Azure Pipelines Customers
item.description: On March 31, 2026, malicious versions of the widely used JavaScript HTTP client library Axios were briefly published to the npm registry as part of a supply chain attack. The affected versions — 1.14.1 and 0.30.4 — included a hidden malicious dependency Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/devops/axios-npm-supply-chain-compromise-guidance-for-azure-pipelines-customers

item.title: Azure MCP Server now available as an MCP Bundle (.mcpb)
item.description: We're excited to announce that the Azure MCP Server is now available as an MCP Bundle (.mcpb). This means you can install the Azure MCP Server into Claude Desktop and other MCP-compatible clients Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/azure-sdk/azure-mcp-server-mcpb-support

item.title: What's new for .NET in Ubuntu 26.04
item.description: Today is launch day for Ubuntu 26.04 (Resolute Raccoon). Congratulations on the release to our friends at Canonical. Each new Ubuntu LTS comes with the latest .NET LTS so that you ca Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/dotnet/whats-new-for-dotnet-in-ubuntu-2604

item.title: LangChain.js for Beginners: A Free Course to Build Agentic AI Apps with JavaScript
item.description: Want to build AI agents with JavaScript that go beyond basic chat completions? Agents that reason, call tools, and pull from knowledge bases on their own? We put together a free, open source course to help you get there. LangChain.js for Beginners is 8 cha Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/blog/langchainjs-for-beginners

item.title: General Availability: Single Sign-On (SSO) from Native Apps to Embedded Web Views in Microsoft Entra External ID Native Authentication
item.description: We’re excited to announce the General Availability (GA) of Single Sign-On (SSO) from Native Apps to Embedded Web Views for Microsoft Entra External ID (EEID) Native Authentication. This release marks a major milestone in delivering end-to-end seamless authentication experiences for modern CIAM applications bridging the gap between Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/identity/native-auth-sso-ga

item.title: CodeAct in Agent Framework: Faster Agents with Fewer Model Turns
item.description: Modern AI agents often aren't bottlenecked by model quality, they are bottlenecked by orchestration overhead. When an agent chains together many small tool calls, each step typically requires a new model turn, driving up latency and token usage. With CodeAct sup Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/agent-framework/codeact-with-hyperlight

item.title: Another crash caused by uninstaller code injection into Explorer
item.description: Some time ago, I noted that any sufficiently advanced uninstaller is indistinguishable from malware.¹ During one of our regular debugging chats, a colleague of mine mentione Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/oldnewthing/20260423-00/?p=112261

item.title: Lessons Learned from Building a Well-Matching Intelligence Layer
item.description: Introduction In the energy industry, a well is a drilled borehole used to extract oil, gas, or other resources. Each well generates data across many stages of its lifecycle—drilling, fluids, subsurface analysis, cementing, and more. Different functional groups, often known as service lines, store this data in their Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/ise/lessons-learned-building-well-matching-intelligence-layer

item.title: Write azd hooks in Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, or .NET
item.description: Hooks are one of the most popular features in azd, and now you can write them in Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, or .NET, not just Bash and PowerShell. What's new? The Azure Developer CLI (azd) hook system now supports four more languages beyond Bash and PowerShell. You can write hook scripts in Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/azure-sdk/azd-multi-language-hooks

item.title: 7 tips to optimize Azure Cosmos DB costs for AI and agentic workloads
item.description: AI apps and agentic workloads expose inefficiencies in your data layer faster than any previous generation of apps. You’re storing embeddings, serving low-latency retrieval, handling bursty traffic from chat and orchestration, and often operating across regions. Done right, Azure Cosmos DB can support these patterns with high performance and Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/cosmosdb/7-tips-to-optimize-azure-cosmos-db-costs-for-ai-and-agentic-workloads

item.title: Optimizing Git policy management at scale
item.description: With just a single improvement in the REST API of Azure DevOps, we achieved a massive reduction in CPU usage and execution time when managing Git policies: 2x less CPU and 10-15x faster execution! This change is already available to all users of Azure DevOps, and it's time to share a bit more d Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/devops/optimizing-git-policy-management-at-scale

item.title: Take the 2026 ISO C++ Developer Survey!
item.description: The 2026 ISO C++ developer survey is now open: 2026 Annual C++ Developer Survey "Lite" : Standard C++ It takes about 10 minutes and directly informs the standardization committee and tooling ven Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/cppblog/take-the-2026-iso-c-developer-survey

item.title: C++ Code Intelligence for GitHub Copilot CLI (Preview)
item.description: We recently brought C++ code understanding tools to GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio and VS C Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/cppblog/c-code-intelligence-for-github-copilot-cli-preview

item.title: Introducing Toolboxes in Foundry
item.description: Available in Public Preview Today Toolbox is a new way to curate, configure, and reuse tools across a Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/foundry/introducing-toolboxes-in-foundry

item.title: From Local to Production: The Complete Developer Journey for Building, Composing, and Deploying AI Agents
item.description: When we launched Microsoft Agent Framework last October, we made a promise: building production-grade AI agents should feel as natural and structured as building any other software. Today, we’re delivering Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/foundry/from-local-to-production-the-complete-developer-journey-for-building-composing-and-deploying-ai-agents

item.title: Introducing the new hosted agents in Foundry Agent Service: secure, scalable compute built for agents
item.description: Agents are already transforming how developers solve problems. Whether it's a coding agent that refactors your repo overnight, a research agent that synthesizes hundreds of documents into a brief, or an ops agent that monitors and remediates infrastructure — the pattern is clear. Developers are building agents that don't just answer question Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/foundry/introducing-the-new-hosted-agents-in-foundry-agent-service-secure-scalable-compute-built-for-agents

item.title: Securing MCP: A Control Plane for Agent Tool Execution
item.description: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is quickly becoming a common way for AI agents to discover and use tools. It provides a consistent interface to databases, APIs, file systems, and third-party services, which makes it easier to plug capabilities into agent workflows. However, MCP standardizes th Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/blog/securing-mcp-a-control-plane-for-agent-tool-execution

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.117
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.117 Read the full article Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/vscode-blog/visual-studio-code-1.117
